steps.title: Turn the inequality into three cutoffs
steps.content: All four answer choices use the same $x$-values, so the fastest method is to compute the maximum allowed $y$ for each one. From $y\lt 6x+2$: when $x=3$, $y\lt 20$; when $x=5$, $y\lt 32$; when $x=7$, $y\lt 44$.

steps.title: Use the Desmos check
steps.content: Graph $y<6x+2$ and plot a table's three points. A table is correct only if all three points land in the shaded region, not on the boundary line. For choice C, the points $(3,16)$, $(5,28)$, and $(7,40)$ are all below the boundary.

steps.title: Eliminate the others
steps.content: Choice A fails because $(3,20)$ is on the boundary, not less than it. Choice B fails because $(5,36)$ is above the allowed cutoff $32$. Choice D fails immediately at $(3,24)$. Only choice C has all three $y$-values below their cutoffs, so C is correct.
